features.summary: Disney reported strong Q2 2022 results with diluted EPS of $1.08, up $0.29 year-over-year. Parks segment delivered exceptional performance with domestic parks showing over 40% per capita spending growth versus 2019, driven by strong demand and yield management strategies. Disney+ added 7.9 million subscribers to reach 138 million globally, keeping the service on track for 230-260 million subscribers by fiscal 2024. Direct-to-consumer losses widened to $900 million due to higher programming costs, while linear networks remained relatively flat with strong sports advertising growth at ESPN.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in achieving Disney+ profitability by fiscal 2024 and maintaining subscriber growth targets despite some moderation in net adds expectations for the second half. The company plans to launch an ad-supported Disney+ tier by end of calendar 2022, expanding to 53 new international markets by Q3. Content spending guidance was slightly reduced to $32 billion from $33 billion for fiscal 2022. Parks demand remains robust with strong forward bookings, though international parks face COVID-related headwinds with potential $350 million impact in Q3.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID impacts on Asian theme parks, with Shanghai Disney closed and Hong Kong Disneyland facing restrictions. Rising inflation pressures across parks operations including labor, fuel, and supply chain costs pose margin challenges. Geopolitical factors may affect Eastern European Disney+ launches, while China theatrical releases remain uncertain since 2019. Direct-to-consumer programming costs continue rising significantly, with Q3 expected to increase over $900 million year-over-year.
features.key_drivers: Domestic parks performance driven by successful yield management and reservation system optimization, enabling sustained per capita spending growth. Disney+ growth supported by strong content slate including Marvel and Star Wars properties, plus expansion into general entertainment content targeting adult demographics. The upcoming ad-supported tier and international expansion represent significant growth catalysts. Sports programming strength at ESPN with strong NBA and NHL playoff ratings driving advertising revenue growth.
